This is becoming a  pattern in AI that makes talking about capabilities challenging.

First, there are overstated claims (like the flubbed Erdos problems last year), then minor wins (AI helps with discovery) then breakthroughs. The first stage feels like (& often is) hype, but…

EvoMapAI 旗下的 autogame-17 项目指控 Nous Research 的 Hermes Agent 抄袭了其发布于 2026 年 2 月 1 日的 Evolver 框架中的关键要素——诸如自我进化、分层记忆及动态技能等——且未给予任何署名致谢。

Nous 的联合创始人 Teknium 对此予以否认，并援引其在 2025 年 7

OpenAI 的 Agents SDK 最近做了一次重要升级，增加了两个关键功能：内置沙箱执行环境和模型原生执行框架（Harness）。这次更新的目标，是帮助开发者更容易地创建安全可靠、能长时间稳定运行的 Agent。

The worst part of building isn’t shipping.

It’s the pile of emails, follow-ups, and random ops work waiting for you right after.

Been using Wingman in beta and waking up to a cleaned up inbox + drafted replies is hard to give up now.

The dominant paradigm in AI today is organized around language and code.

But physical AI is maturing concurrently, and the pace of progress over the past 18 months suggests that new fields could soon enter a scaling regime of their own.

a16z's Oliver Hsu on robot learning, https://t.co/orUo1xDDGk

If you're an AI creator, you know the drill: Nano Banana for frame refs, Kling 3 Omni for character consistency, then Photoshop, Premiere and After Effects to stitch it all together.

What if one AI agent could orchestrate all of it?

That's what my team at Adobe is building. https://t.co/ZAMlJe3wqF
